hawkish_phrases: the Committee decided to raise the target range for the federal funds rate to 1 to 1-1/4 percent
The Committee expects that economic conditions will evolve in a manner that will warrant gradual increases in the federal funds rate
the stance of monetary policy remained accommodative, supporting some further strengthening in labor market conditions
The Committee currently expects to begin implementing a balance sheet normalization program this year
a substantial undershooting of the longer-run normal rate of unemployment could pose an appreciable upside risk to inflation
dovish_phrases: Inflation on a 12-month basis had declined recently and was running somewhat below 2 percent
Recent readings on headline and core PCE price inflation had come in lower than participants had expected
several participants expressed concern that progress toward the Committee's 2 percent longer-run inflation objective might have slowed
some participants emphasized downside risks, particularly in light of the recent low readings on inflation
the federal funds rate is likely to remain, for some time, below levels that are expected to prevail in the longer run
summary: The Committee voted 8-1 to raise the federal funds rate by 25bp to 1.00-1.25% with Kashkari dissenting, announced plans to begin balance sheet normalization in 2017, and reaffirmed gradual further rate increases despite inflation running below 2%.
